This might seem weird to you at first, but when was the last time you called up the home shopping network to negotiate something that was 1995? Numbers have a psychological significance beyond just value. Certain numbers seem less negotiable than others. Numbers that end with a zero seem less important, as if there wasn't any thought behind them. They feel like temporary placeholders or guesstimates that can be easily changed. However, when you throw out a number that isn't rounded, for example, $105,357, it gives the number more authority. They will believe that you came to this figure through careful calculations. Numbers that end in odd numbers are perceived as more permanent and immovable. Numbers that end with a zero seem less important, as if there wasn't any thought behind them.
